data.body:
Consolidating 049c-df32 (duplicate) into this ticket. Additional signals from the 2026-04-16 evidence session:
- 21 agent-* worktrees present, all locked; 6 had uncommitted scratch files; 1 had an unmerged RED-test commit.
- 82 worktree-agent-* branches remained after manual worktree removal — branch cleanup is fully orphaned from worktree cleanup.
- Of the 82 branches, 75 were ancestors of main (safe merged) and 7 were unmerged; all 7 referenced tickets (3601-f085, 615f-fad3, 639d-fe1b, 6b3b-41b0) that were already closed, i.e. work was superseded via other paths.
- User-supplied correlation: the sessions that produced these worktrees had auto-compact events. Recorded as evidence, not cause.

Known-issue match: none in KNOWN-ISSUES.md.
Hypothesis surface for investigation:
(a) claude-safe _offer_worktree_cleanup scope: does it cover .claude/worktrees/agent-* or only the session worktree path?
(b) worktree-cleanup.sh scope: does it traverse agent-* worktrees, and does it delete the backing branch alongside the worktree?
(c) harvest-worktree.sh post-harvest action: after successful harvest, does it remove the agent worktree or leave it locked?
(d) auto-compact interaction: whether auto-compact drops the in-progress orchestrator state that would trigger post-harvest cleanup.
